'''Tresina Lobi''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Chev]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] during the war against the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. She had a noted ability for blending into crowds.

==Biography==
===The Yuuzhan Vong War===
During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], Lobi was [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] [[Thrynni Vae]] as her [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]]. When Vae went missing on a mission to [[Duro/Legends|Duro]] a year into the conflict, she sought out [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] to help find her. Sadly, it was discovered that Vae had been killed by the treacherous [[Peace Brigade]]. Despite the loss of her apprentice, Lobi was still able to determine that supplies for refugees on Duro were being scuttled to the floating [[Urrdorf City]].<ref name="BP">''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]''</ref>

[[28 ABY/Legends|Four years]] into the war, she was chosen by Master Skywalker to sit on the [[High Council (Galactic Alliance)|High Council]], where she was a strong voice for the [[New Jedi Order]].<ref name="DW">''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]''</ref> During the [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar|final battle]] against the Yuuzhan Vong on [[Coruscant/Legends|Yuuzhan'tar]], Lobi was one of many [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] to defend [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]] from [[Alpha Red (pathogen)|Alpha Red]].<ref name="TUF">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref>

===The Dark Nest Crisis===
Five years after the war, Lobi went to find the [[Gorog|Dark Nest]] with [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], and [[Battle of Kr|fought]] against the [[Gorog]] [[Killik/Legends|Killiks]] on [[Kr]].<ref name="DNI">''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]''</ref> She sided with [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] during the debate on whether he should take control of the Jedi Order in Luke's absence.<ref name="DNII">''[[Dark Nest II: The Unseen Queen]]''</ref> During the final phase of the Killik expansion, Lobi led a [[StealthX]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of Jedi fighters against [[Lomi Plo]] and her Dark Nest where she suffered crash burns. It is unknown if this was a result of a crash.<ref name="DNIII">''[[Dark Nest III: The Swarm War]]''</ref>

===Second Galactic Civil War===
{{Quote|You are good Master Lobi—but not that good.|Alema Rar|<ref name="Tempest">''[[Legacy of the Force: Tempest]]''</ref>}}
During the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], Lobi was assigned to watch [[Ben Skywalker]] closely; particularly after it was learned that [[Lumiya]] had returned. Lobi's task led her to the [[Fellowship Plaza]] in the heart of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant's]] Senatorial District, where she eavesdropped on a conversation between [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] and Ben Skywalker, followed shortly after by a revealing conversation between Jacen Solo and Lumiya.<ref name="Tempest" />

Before she could complete the transmission to Luke that Lumiya was after Ben, she was detected by [[Alema Rar]], who was also eavesdropping on Jacen Solo. A fierce [[Duel in the Fellowship Plaza|duel]] occurred in which Lobi severed Lumiya's [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] arm and was able to hold her own against the two women. Lumiya and Rar eventually overwhelmed the Jedi Master; once Lumiya's [[Lightwhip/Legends|lightwhip]] severed her legs from behind, Lobi was swiftly decapitated by Rar, who felt the Jedi Master had fought well and suffered enough. Her body was found the next morning by a [[gardener droid]].<ref name="Tempest" />

===Legacy===
In [[44 ABY]], [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] glimpsed Lobi in the [[Lake of Apparitions]] while seeking his deceased wife [[Mara Jade Skywalker]], but did not stop to talk with her.<ref name="Apocalypse">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Apocalypse]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Lobi was mistakenly identified and described as a [[Chevin/Legends|Chevin]] in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]''. The [[2002]] ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' article ''[[Who's Who in the New Jedi Order]]'' stated that Lobi was the first Chev to develop Force-sensitivity. However, this was later overruled by the [[2004]] [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|role-playing]] adventure ''[[A Mon Alone]]'', which featured a Force-sensitive Chev named [[Drik]], who was active during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].
